description: Friend Profile Actions - Complete relationship management from the profile page with cascade deletion, request cancellation, and accept/decline actions

userStories.title: Update BurnBuddyManagement.tla for cascade deletion and cancel request
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the BurnBuddyManagement TLA+ spec to model cascade friend→buddy deletion and sender-initiated request cancellation so correctness is verified before implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: In specs/tla/burn-buddies/BurnBuddyManagement.tla, update the DeleteFriendship action to also remove the burn buddy relationship for the pair (not just pending requests): add burnBuddies' = burnBuddies \ {{u1, u2}} and change UNCHANGED burnBuddies to include the new assignment

Add a new CancelBuddyRequest(from, to) action that allows the sender (fromUid) to withdraw a pending request — preconditions: request exists with status=pending and fromUid=from, postcondition: request removed from buddyRequests set, UNCHANGED friends and burnBuddies
Add CancelBuddyRequest to the Next state relation: \/ \E from, to \in Uid : CancelBuddyRequest(from, to)
Add a new invariant NoBuddyWithoutFriendship == \A bb \in burnBuddies : bb \in friends, and add it to SafetyInvariant conjunction

Run TLC model checker on BurnBuddyManagement.tla and confirm zero invariant violations (use: cd specs/tla/burn-buddies && java -cp /usr/local/lib/tla2tools.jar tlc2.TLC BurnBuddyManagement or the project's TLC runner if available)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing DeleteFriendship action (line 140-147) already removes pending buddy requests but has UNCHANGED burnBuddies — change this to also cascade-delete the active burn buddy. The CancelBuddyRequest action is similar to DeclineBuddyRequest but fires when the canceller equals fromUid instead of toUid.

userStories.title: Update CrossDomainInvariants.tla for cascade deletion and CDI-8
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the cross-domain invariants spec to model cascade friend→buddy deletion and add the new invariant that no burn buddy can exist without an active friendship.
acceptanceCriteria: In specs/tla/CrossDomainInvariants.tla, update DeleteFriendship action (line 151-155) to also cascade-delete the burn buddy: add burnBuddies' = burnBuddies \ {{u1, u2}} and update the UNCHANGED tuple to remove burnBuddies from it

Remove or update the CDI-6 comment (lines 376-381) that says 'burn buddy relationships persist after friendship deletion — BY DESIGN'. Replace with a comment explaining burn buddies are now cascade-deleted when friendship is removed
Add new invariant CDI-8: NoBuddyWithoutFriendship == \A bb \in burnBuddies : bb \in friends
Add NoBuddyWithoutFriendship to the CrossDomainInvariant conjunction (line 394-396)
Run TLC model checker on CrossDomainInvariants.tla and confirm zero invariant violations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CrossDomainInvariants.tla currently documents that burn buddies persist after friendship deletion as 'by design' (CDI-6). This PRD changes that behavior. The DeleteFriendship action at line 151-155 currently has UNCHANGED burnBuddies — this must change.

userStories.title: Add relationship IDs and friendship status to ProfileStats shared type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ProfileStats shared type to include friendshipStatus, pendingBuddyRequestId, and burnBuddyId so the frontend can call the correct action endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: In packages/shared/src/types.ts, add friendshipStatus: 'friends' to the ProfileStats interface
In packages/shared/src/types.ts, add pendingBuddyRequestId: string | null to the ProfileStats interface
In packages/shared/src/types.ts, add burnBuddyId: string | null to the ProfileStats interface
Run cd packages/shared && yarn build to compile the shared package
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The shared package must be built after type changes since the API and web app resolve @burnbuddy/shared from compiled dist/ output. Run yarn build in packages/shared after editing types.ts.

userStories.title: Update profile stats API to return relationship IDs and friendship status
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the GET /users/:uid/profile endpoint to return friendshipStatus, pendingBuddyRequestId, and burnBuddyId so I can render action buttons and call the correct endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /users/:uid/profile response includes friendshipStatus: 'friends' (always present since the endpoint requires friendship)
When buddyRelationshipStatus is 'pending_sent' or 'pending_received', the response includes pendingBuddyRequestId with the Firestore document ID of the pending request
When buddyRelationshipStatus is 'buddies', the response includes burnBuddyId with the Firestore document ID (format: uid1_uid2 with sorted UIDs)
When buddyRelationshipStatus is 'none', both pendingBuddyRequestId and burnBuddyId are null
When buddyRelationshipStatus is 'buddies', pendingBuddyRequestId is null; when 'pending_sent'/'pending_received', burnBuddyId is null
Existing profile endpoint tests in services/api/src/routes/users.test.ts are updated to assert the new fields
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The profile endpoint is in services/api/src/routes/users.ts. It already queries burnBuddyRequests for sent/received pending requests (around line 499-532) — extract the document ID from those query results. For burnBuddyId, find the matching burn buddy document. Build shared package first: cd packages/shared && yarn build.

userStories.title: Add cancel burn buddy request API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cancel a burn buddy request I've sent so I can change my mind before the recipient responds.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint DELETE /burn-buddies/requests/:id is added to services/api/src/routes/burn-buddies.ts
Only the sender (fromUid === req.user.uid) can cancel their own pending request
Returns 401 if not authenticated
Returns 404 if request document doesn't exist
Returns 403 if the authenticated user is not the sender (fromUid)
Returns 409 if the request status is not 'pending'
Returns 204 on successful deletion (deletes the request document from Firestore)
Unit tests are added to services/api/src/routes/burn-buddies.test.ts covering: 401 auth required, 404 not found, 403 not sender, 409 not pending, 204 success
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the existing test pattern: vi.hoisted mocks, buildApp(), supertest. The burn-buddies route file already has GET/POST/PUT/DELETE patterns to follow. The request document ID is from the burnBuddyRequests Firestore collection.

userStories.title: Update friend deletion API to cascade-delete burn buddy and pending requests
userStories.description: As a user, when I remove a friend who is also my burn buddy, both relationships should be removed atomically.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /friends/:uid in services/api/src/routes/friends.ts checks for and deletes any active burn buddy document between the two users (query burnBuddies collection for the sorted UID pair)
DELETE /friends/:uid also deletes any pending burn buddy requests between the two users (query burnBuddyRequests for pending requests in both directions)
Use a Firestore batch write to delete the friend document, burn buddy document (if exists), and pending requests (if any) atomically
Existing friend deletion test (204 success) still passes

New tests added: friend-only deletion (no burn buddy exists — just friend removed), friend+buddy cascade (both friend and burn buddy deleted), friend+pending-request cascade (friend and pending buddy request deleted)
All API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing DELETE /friends/:uid handler is in friends.ts around line 230-245. Use getDb() for Firestore access. The burn buddy document ID format is ${uid1}_${uid2} with sorted UIDs. Use db.batch() for atomic writes.

userStories.title: Add Remove Friend and Remove Burn Buddy buttons to profile page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a friend's profile, I want buttons to remove the friend or end the burn buddy relationship with appropriate confirmation dialogs.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Remove Friend' button with destructive/red styling appears on all friend profiles in apps/web/src/app/(main)/profile/[uid]/page.tsx
When the friend is NOT a burn buddy, clicking Remove Friend shows confirmation: 'Remove [displayName] as a friend?'
When the friend IS a burn buddy, clicking Remove Friend shows confirmation: 'Removing [displayName] as a friend will also end your burn buddy relationship. Are you sure?'
On Remove Friend confirmation, calls DELETE /friends/:uid (using the profile uid from URL params) via the API client in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts, then redirects to /friends
A 'Remove Burn Buddy' button appears only when buddyRelationshipStatus === 'buddies'
Clicking Remove Burn Buddy shows confirmation: 'End burn buddy relationship with [displayName]? You will remain friends.'
On Remove Burn Buddy confirmation, calls DELETE /burn-buddies/:id (using burnBuddyId from profile stats) via the API client, then refreshes the profile data
Buttons are disabled and show loading state while API calls are in flight
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The profile page is at apps/web/src/app/(main)/profile/[uid]/page.tsx. It already has conditional rendering based on buddyRelationshipStatus. Use window.confirm() for confirmation dialogs. The API client is in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts — follow existing patterns (getIdToken + fetch with Bearer header). The burnBuddyId field was added to the profile response in US-004.

userStories.title: Add accept/decline/cancel buttons for pending burn buddy requests on profile page
userStories.description: As a user viewing a friend's profile, I want to accept/decline incoming burn buddy requests or cancel my outgoing requests directly from the profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: When buddyRelationshipStatus === 'pending_received', show 'Accept' (primary styled) and 'Decline' (secondary styled) buttons instead of just the 'Request Received' badge
'Accept' calls POST /burn-buddies/requests/:id/accept (using pendingBuddyRequestId from profile stats) and refreshes profile to show burn buddy status
'Decline' calls POST /burn-buddies/requests/:id/decline (using pendingBuddyRequestId) and refreshes profile to show buddyRelationshipStatus: 'none'
When buddyRelationshipStatus === 'pending_sent', show 'Cancel Request' button (secondary styled) instead of just the 'Request Pending' badge
Clicking 'Cancel Request' shows confirmation: 'Cancel burn buddy request to [displayName]?'
On Cancel Request confirmation, calls DELETE /burn-buddies/requests/:id (using pendingBuddyRequestId) and refreshes profile to show 'none' status with 'Request Burn Buddy' button
All buttons are disabled and show loading state while API calls are in flight
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The profile page currently shows static badges for pending_sent and pending_received states (around lines 113-133). Replace these with interactive buttons. The pendingBuddyRequestId field was added to the profile response in US-004. The cancel endpoint was added in US-005. Use the API client pattern from apps/web/src/lib/api.ts.
